name: HMS Fir (T 129)
events: 17 Feb 1943 At 1820 A/17, HMS Umbra (Lt. S.L.C. Maydon, DSO, RN) arrived at Plymouth.

At 1237 A/17, she had made rendezvous with HMS Fir (T/Skr. W.H. Buley, RNR) at the end of the swept channel to Plymouth. HMS Umbra had arrived early at the rendezvous and had signalled to the Lizard signalled station that she would proceeded towards the end of the swept channel and meet her escort there.

HMS Umbra started her refit at the Devonport Dockyard on 24 February 1943. Refit was completed in late July 1943. [As no log is available for July 1943 some details for this month may be missing.] (1)
15 Nov 1943 HMS Unbending (Lt. J.D. Martin, DSC, RN) proceeded from Plymouth to Dartmouth. Also log trials were carried out. She was escorted by HMS Fir (T/Skr. W.H. Buley, RNR). (2)

22 Mar 1945 HMS Fir (Skr.Lt. W.H. Buley, RNR) picks up 49 survivors from the British merchant Empire Kingley that was torpedoed and sunk by German U-boat U-315 northwest of Lands End in position 50°08'N, 05°51'W.
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
